entities.name: PLANT DEFENSINS
entities.description: Small, cysteine-rich cationic antimicrobial peptides that possess biological activity towards a broad range of pathogenic organisms.

entities.name: PATHOGENIC ORGANISMS
entities.description: Organisms that cause disease in plants and are targeted by plant defensins.

entities.name: PLANT DEFENSE
entities.description: The first line of protection in plants against pathogens and injury.

entities.name: PLANT TISSUES
entities.description: Various plant parts such as seedlings, leaves, tubers, flowers, pods, roots, and fruits where defensins are expressed.

entities.name: PATHOGEN ATTACK
entities.description: The process of a pathogen attacking a plant, which can induce defensin expression.

entities.name: INJURY
entities.description: Physical damage to plant tissues that triggers a systemic defense response.

entities.name: SYSTEMIC DEFENSE RESPONSE
entities.description: A plant-wide response to pathogen attack or injury involving plant defensins.

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Non-living environmental stressors that can induce the expression of plant defensins.

entities.name: HORMONE SIGNALING PATHWAYS
entities.description: Biological pathways that regulate the expression of plant defensins.

entities.name: TRANSGENIC TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: Technology used to overexpress plant defensins in targeted tissues of plants.

entities.name: ANTIFUNGAL ACTIVITY
entities.description: The broadspectrum ability of plant defensins to inhibit fungal growth.

entities.name: TISSUE SPECIFIC PROMOTERS
entities.description: DNA sequences used to control the expression of plant defensins in specific plant tissues.

entities.name: DEVELOPMENTAL PROCESSES
entities.description: Biological processes of plant growth and development in which plant defensins play a role.
